children.heading: International military education and training
children.label: a International military education and training

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary for each of the fiscal years 2010 through 2014 for assistance under chapter 5 of part II of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ( et seq.; relating to international military education and training) for Pakistan, including expanded international military education and training (commonly known as “E–IMET”).22 U.S.C. 2347

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: 2 Use of funds
children.text: It is the sense of Congress that a substantial amount of funds made available to carry out this subsection for a fiscal year should be used to pay for courses of study and training in counterinsurgency and civil-military relations.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary for each of the fiscal years 2010 through 2014 for grant assistance under (relating to the Foreign Military Financing program) for the purchase of defense articles, defense services, and military education and training for Pakistan.section 2763 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A significant portion of the amount made available to carry out this subsection for a fiscal year shall be for the purchase of defense articles, defense services, and military education and training for activities relating to counterinsurgency and counterterrorism operations in Pakistan.

children.heading: Sense of Congress
children.label: B Sense of Congress
children.text: It is the sense of Congress that a significant majority of funds made available to carry out this subsection for a fiscal year should be used for the purpose described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Additional authority
children.label: 3 Additional authority
children.text: Except as provided in sections 2753 and 2799aa–1 of this title, the second section 620J of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 (as added by ) [], and any provision of an Act making appropriations for the Department of State, foreign operations, and related programs that restricts assistance to the government of any country whose duly elected head of government is deposed by military coup or decree, and except as otherwise provided in this subchapter, amounts authorized to be made available to carry out paragraph (2) for fiscal years 2010 and 2011 are authorized to be made available notwithstanding any other provision of law.1Public Law 110–16122 U.S.C. 2378dSee References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: 4 Definitions
children.text: In this section, the terms “defense articles”, “defense services”, and “military education and training” have the meaning given such terms in section 644 of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ().22 U.S.C. 2403

children.heading: Sense of Congress
children.label: c Sense of Congress
children.text: It is the sense of Congress that the United States should facilitate Pakistan’s establishment of a program to provide reconstruction assistance, including through Pakistan’s military as appropriate, in areas damaged by combat operations.

children.heading: Exchange program between military and civilian personnel of Pakistan and certain other countries
children.label: d Exchange program between military and civilian personnel of Pakistan and certain other countries
A
children.text: military and civilian personnel of Pakistan; and

children.label: i
children.text: military and civilian personnel of countries determined by the Secretary of State to be in the process of consolidating and strengthening a democratic form of government; or

children.label: ii
children.text: military and civilian personnel of North Atlantic Treaty Organization member countries,

children.heading: Elements of program
children.label: 2 Elements of program
children.text: The program authorized under paragraph (1) may include conferences, seminars, exchanges, and other events, distribution of publications and reimbursements of expenses of foreign military personnel participating in the program, including transportation, translation and administrative expenses.

children.heading: Role of nongovernmental organizations
children.label: 3 Role of nongovernmental organizations
children.text: Amounts authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section for a fiscal year are authorized to be made available for nongovernmental organizations to facilitate the implementation of the program authorized under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary for each of the fiscal years 2010 through 2014 to carry out the program established by this subsection.
